quoteFirst: ] About dark they began to spread, and got beyond the control of the brigade on duty within the city. The whole of Woods' division was brought in, but it was found impossible to check the flames, which, by midnight, had become unmanageable, and raged until
quoteLast: , when the wind subsiding, they were got under control.
title: Historical Essays
author: Rhodes, James Ford, 1848-1927

quoteFirst: Their daily routine was somewhat as follows.
quoteLast: the inferior of the two big merchants would wake up and call to the head cook that it was time to get up, the latter would then give a loud shout, which sounded like "Chou-chou, chou-chou, chou-chou-ou-ou!" and which was promptly echoed by men on guard all through the camp.
title: Through Unknown Tibet
author: Wellby, M. S. (Montagu Sinclair), 1866-1900

quoteFirst: So, being willing to see what had happened in Gwillimbury since 1837, I took a waggon and the land road, and went off as day broke, or rather before it broke,
quoteLast: , in a deep gray mist. The waggon should be described, as it is the best _voiture_ in Western Canada.
title: Canada and the Canadians, Vol. 1
author: Bonnycastle, Richard Henry, Sir, 1791-1847

quoteFirst: On April 15, 1920, I was picked up
quoteLast: at my boarding house, 181 North Main St., Providence, by four Italians who came in a Hudson five-passenger open touring car.
title: Facing the chair
author: Dos Passos, John, 1896-1970; Debs, Eugene V. (Eugene Victor), 1855-1926 [Contributor]; France, Anatole, 1844-1924 [Contributor]

quoteFirst: If you or Bob could hunt around and find a box of the right sort that would hold, say, 50 pounds of meat together with 50 pounds of dry ice, and if shipping meat to children is not contrary to law, I think something could be done to relieve you and the rest of the children in this part of the country. . . I am too busy on all this land we have to be finding out all the details and hunting up all the containers. I get up from
quoteLast: and get to bed from 9 to 10 p.m.
title: Epistles from Pap: Letters from the man known as 'The Will Rogers of Indiana'
author: Durham, Andrew Everett, 1882-1954; Durham, J. Frank, 1915-2011 [Compiler]; Hay, Douglas (Douglas N.) [Editor]

quoteLast: gun fire again commenced, and the stream of wounded began to come down from the crest to the foot of the hill and beyond.
title: My Experiences at Nan Shan and Port Arthur with the Fifth East Siberian Rifles
author: Tret'iakov, Nikolaĭ Aleksandrovich, 1854-1917; Baker, F. Nolan [Editor]; Alford, Arthur Cameron Rimington, 1882-1954 [Translator]

quoteFirst: ) _South Bishop_ L.H., several birds flying round the lantern from midnight to 3 A.M.; eight Blackbirds, two Starlings, two Larks, and three Thrushes killed. _Nash_ (_E._) L.H., a Jacksnipe killed at 3 A.M. Blackbirds from
quoteLast: , three killed; Redwings from 3 to 4 A.M., six killed; Skylarks from 3 to 4 A.M., two killed; Song Thrushes from 3 to 4 A.M., four killed; Snipe, one killed at 3.30 A.M. (E., 3; B. C. M.) _Breaksea_ L.V., Thrushes and Starlings from midnight (17th) to 4 A.M. (E.N.E., 4; O. M.) _Burnham_, the first Grey Wagtail seen.
title:
Report on the Migration of Birds in the Spring and Autumn of 1887. Ninth Report

quoteFirst: He said he had, and that these sounds were just like those of a battle.  An interval of quiet then ensued, when again,
quoteLast: , arose other similar explosions, but I still remained in doubt whether the enemy was engaged in blowing up his own magazines, or whether General Slocum had not felt forward, and become engaged in a real battle.
title: Memoirs of General William T. Sherman — Volume 2
author: Sherman, William T. (William Tecumseh), 1820-1891

quoteFirst: Then a friendly orderly shouldered my kit and led me into this tent. Here I stripped off everything, packed all my kit in a bundle, washed, put on a clean suit of pyjamas, and at
quoteLast: was lying in this delicious bed, dead-beat, but blissfully comfortable. Oddly, I couldn't sleep, but lay in a dreamy trance, smoking cigarettes, with a beatific red-caped vision hovering about in the half light.
title: In the Ranks of the C.I.V.
author: Childers, Erskine, 1870-1922

quoteLast: we sighted a Zeppelin, which passed over astern of us.
title: From Snotty to Sub
author: Forester, Wolston B. C. W. (Wolston Beaumont Charles Weld); Forester, Elspeth Lascelles, -1931 [Editor]
